reason: Adding the nosuid mount option to /home is a standard security hardening practice that prevents execution of setuid programs in user home directories. It is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage because SUID/SGID permissions are not typically required in /home. The provided remediation script includes platform-specific checks to avoid issues in containerized or ostree-based environments.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; If setuid executables exist in /home, they will become non-executable, potentially breaking legacy applications that depend on them.
